content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcar Pnacrrcns CouvrrssroN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95811-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
July 17,2000
Sheryl Z. White, President
Statecraft, Inc.
8618 Nottingham Place
La Jolla" CA 92037-2126
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-00-140
Dear Ms. White:
You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").I
QUESTION
Are major donor and independent expenditure committees required to disclose
subvendor information on the Form 461?
CONCLUSION
Yes. Every committee, including major donor and independent expenditure
committees, must include subvendor information when disclosing reportable
expenditures.
FACTS
Statecraft, Inc. is in the process of upgrading its campaign disclosure software to
include revisions to the Form 46l,Major Donor and Independent Expenditure Committee
Campaign Statement, and to create the electronic output file for upload to the Office of
the Secretary of State.
' Gouemment Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections
18109-18995, of the California Code of Regulations.


Sheryl Z. White
Our File No. A-00-140
Page z
AIIALYSIS
Under the Act's campaign disclosure provisions (sections 84100 et seq.),
committees are required to file periodic reports disclosing contributions made and
received and expenditures made in connection with state and local elections. The term
"committee" is defined in section 82013 as a person or combination of persons that does
any of the following:
"...(a) Receives contributions totaling one
thousand dollars ($1,000) or more in a calendar year.
(b) Makes independent expenditures totaling one
thousand dollars ($1,000) or more in a calendar year; or
(c) Makes contributions totaling ten thousand
dollars ($10,000) or more in a calendar year to or at the
behest of candidates or commifiees."
Your question relates to individuals and entities that incur campaign reporting
obligations under sections 82013(b) ("independent expenditure committees") and
820 1 3(c) ("major donor committees").
Section 84211specifies the information that must be disclosed on campaign
disclosure reports. For expenditures of $100 or more made to a single payee, committees
are required to provide the name and address of the payee as well as the amount and a
description of the payment. (Section 84211(i).) This same information is required to be
disclosed for "each person, if different than the payee, who has provided consideration
for an expenditue of one hundred dollars ($100) or more during the period covered by
the campaign statement." (Section842li(iX6).) These types of payments are commonly
referred to as "subvendor payments."
In addition, Section 84303 provides
"No expenditure shall be made, other than overhead or
normal operating expenses, by an agent or independent
contractor, including but not limited to an advertising
agerrcy, on behalf of or for the benefit of any candidate 6r
commtttee unless it is reported by the candidate or
committee as if the expenditure were made directly by the
candidate or committee. The agent or independent
contractor shall make known to the candidate or committee
all information required to be reported by this section."
(Emphasis added.)


I
Sheryl Z. White
Our File No. A-00-140
Page 3
There is nothing in the Act or Commission regulations limiting the application of
sections 8421t(i and 84303 to any particular type of committee. However, the
Commission has previously advised that for independent expenditure and major donor
committees, only contributions, urs defined in section 82015 and Regulation 18215, and
independent expendittues, trs defined in section 82031 and Regulation 18225, need be
reported. (Nielsen Advice Letter, No. 4-76-520; Bagatelos Advice Letter, No. I-87-234.)
Likewise, these committees are only required to report subvendor information for
payments that are contributions or independent expenditures.
If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel
Con-{,,-il^a (ma
By
Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

sections.question:
Are major donor and independent expenditure committees required to disclose
subvendor information on the Form 461?

Yes. Every committee, including major donor and independent expenditure
committees, must include subvendor information when disclosing reportable
expenditures.

Sheryl Z. White
